data.title: DOJ Closes Antitrust Probe Into $110 Billion Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. Discovery Merger
data.description: The US Justice Department has closed its antitrust probe into the $110 billion merger of Paramount Skydance Corp. and Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the decision.

data.title: STOCKS | Gerber Calls SpaceX-Tesla Merger a ‘Forgone Conclusion’
data.description: Ross Gerber, president and CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth & Investment Management, said he sees a merger combining SpaceX and Tesla as a “forgone conclusion.” According to Bloomberg, Gerber said the prospect has been supporting Tesla’s stock as investors wait for a chance to own SpaceX, adding that investors who are uncomfortable with Elon Musk’s control of both companies should avoid the shares.

data.title: Guitar Center Expects About $16 Million in Tariff Refunds After Duties Lifted
data.description: Guitar Center Inc. expects to receive about $16 million in tariff refunds after some import duties were lifted, according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Thoreau Group in Advanced Talks to Buy Ensemble Health Partners for About $12 Billion
data.description: Private-equity executive Matt Holt’s investment firm Thoreau Group is in advanced talks to acquire Ensemble Health Partners in a deal valuing the company at about $12 billion. The discussions are ongoing and no final agreement has been reached, according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Oil Drops to Lowest Since Early Iran War as Hormuz Flows Rise
data.description: Oil fell to the lowest since the early days of the Iran war on signs that flows through the Strait of Hormuz are rising. According to Bloomberg, the decline also followed progress toward an interim peace deal.

data.title: Standard Chartered sees SpaceX IPO, possible U.S.-Iran peace deal as crypto catalysts
data.description: Standard Chartered senior market analyst Geoffrey Kendrick said the SpaceX IPO and a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal could be the two catalysts that end the recent crypto selloff, according to CoinDesk.

data.title: S&P Expects Iraq’s Fiscal and External Positions to Remain Under Pressure in 2026
data.description: S&P said it expected Iraq’s fiscal position and balance of payments to remain under pressure in 2026. According to Jin10, the statement did not provide further details or specific figures.

data.title: S&P 500 Trades Near 7,421 After SpaceX’s Record $75 Billion IPO
data.description: The S&P 500 (SPX) traded near 7,421 after SpaceX completed the largest IPO in history, with the index correcting from its record high of 7,620.90 as the $75 billion debut absorbs liquidity. SpaceX sold 555.56 million shares at $135 each, valuing the company at nearly $1.77 trillion, according to BeInCrypto, and began trading on Nasdaq under the SPCX ticker on Friday, June 12. The deal was more than three times oversubscribed, with retail investors taking close to 30% of the allocation. S&P Dow Jones Indices kept eligibility rules unchanged in early June, meaning SPCX cannot join the S&P 500 for at least 12 months.

data.title: Traders’ Bullish Bets on the U.S. Dollar Hit Highest Level Since February 2025, CFTC Data Show
data.description:
Traders’ bullish sentiment toward the U.S. dollar has climbed to its highest level in more than a year, as the Middle East war reinforced the dollar’s role as a safe-haven currency. According to ChainCatcher, citing Jinshi, U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission data show that as of June 9, hedge funds, asset managers, and other speculators held a combined $27.8 billion in bullish U.S. dollar positions, the highest level since February 2025.

The positioning shift indicates that bullish expectations for the world’s main reserve currency have continued to build.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Traders Turn Most Bullish on US Dollar in Over a Year on Middle East War
data.description: Traders turned the most optimistic on the US dollar in more than a year as the war in the Middle East supported the currency’s haven status. In more than a year, according to Bloomberg, this marked the strongest shift toward bullish dollar positioning.

data.title: STOCKS | Fund Managers Cut Net Long Positions in CME S&P 500 Futures, CFTC Data Show
data.description:
U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission data showed that, in the week ended June 9, stock fund managers reduced their net long positions in CME S&P 500 index futures by 5,095 contracts to 980,112 contracts.

According to Jin10, equity-related speculative traders reduced their net short positions in CME S&P 500 index futures by 48,536 contracts to 437,047 contracts.

data.title: Oil Extends Slide on Iran Diplomacy Hopes; Stocks Rise as SpaceX Jumps in Trading Debut
data.description: Oil extended its decline as hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough to end the war in Iran lifted risk appetite and pushed stocks higher. In addition, according to Bloomberg, Wall Street’s gains were also supported by SpaceX’s strong trading debut.

data.title: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce Says She Will Leave Agency After Nearly 30 Years in Washington
data.description:
SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce delivered farewell remarks at the U.S. Chamber of Commerce Capital Markets Summit and said she is leaving the agency after nearly 30 years in Washington.

According to NS3.AI, Peirce confirmed her departure during the event.

Her exit will leave the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission with two commissioners.

data.title: STOCKS | Tesla Investor Merz Says She Skipped SpaceX IPO on Expectation of Musk Tie-Up
data.description: Alexandra Merz, a longtime Tesla Inc. investor, said she deliberately avoided buying shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering because she expects Elon Musk to move quickly to combine the two companies. According to Bloomberg, Merz said she expects Musk to act quickly on a combination between Tesla and SpaceX.

data.title: STOCKS | Roku Shares Jump 19% on Report It Is Considering a Sale
data.description:
Roku shares rose 19% after a report said the company was considering a sale.

According to Jin10, the report said Roku was weighing a potential sale.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Comex Gold, Silver, And Copper Net Long Positions Fall In Week Ended June 9, CFTC Data Show
data.description:
Speculators reduced net long positions in COMEX gold, silver, and copper in the week ended June 9, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission said.

According to Jin10, the CFTC reported that COMEX gold speculators cut net long positions by 7,681 contracts to 103,660.

The CFTC also said COMEX silver speculators reduced net long positions by 639 contracts to 9,794.

In copper, the CFTC reported that COMEX copper speculators lowered net long positions by 6,004 contracts to 71,127.

data.title: France Winter Wheat, Barley Yields Seen Slightly Better Than Last Season, Intercereales Says
data.description: France’s winter soft wheat and barley yields appear slightly better than last season despite adverse weather, according to Benoit Pietrement, president of industry group Intercereales. In comments reported by Bloomberg, Pietrement said the outlook has improved even as weather conditions have been unfavorable.

data.title: Former Treasury Secretary Jack Lew Discusses Social Security Trust Fund Outlook
data.description:
Former US Treasury Secretary Jack Lew joined Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on “Bloomberg Money.”

The latest annual Social Security Trustees report moved up the projected date when reserves run out to the fourth quarter of 2032, according to Bloomberg, and at that point the program would have enough to pay 78% of scheduled benefits.

data.title: STOCKS | Roku in Talks to Sell Itself, Sources Say
data.description: Roku Inc. is in talks to sell itself, people with knowledge of the matter said. According to Bloomberg, the discussions are ongoing and no deal is guaranteed.

data.title: Times Square Crystal Ball to Rise at U.S. Stock Market Close, Report Says
data.description:
A report said the crystal ball in New York’s Times Square will rise at the close of the U.S. stock market as a tribute to SpaceX.

According to Jin10, The Wall Street Journal cited people familiar with the matter as saying the ball will be raised when U.S. equities finish trading.
